title: YesBut: A High-Quality Annotated Multimodal Dataset for evaluating Satire Comprehension capability of Vision-Language Models
research_questions: Whether existing Vision-Language Models are able to decipher satire in images.
How well SOTA VL Models perform on Satirical Image Detection, Satirical Image Understanding, and Satirical Image Completion in zero-shot and zero-shot Chain-of-Thought settings.
Whether the YesBut dataset, with two sub-images, little text, and multiple artistic styles, provides a challenging benchmark for multimodal satire and humor comprehension.

methods_proposed.name: YesBut dataset
methods_proposed.summary: A high-quality annotated multimodal dataset of two-panel satirical and non-satirical images spanning colorized sketches, 2D stick figures, and 3D stick figures. Satirical images include left-sub-image, right-sub-image, and overall punchline descriptions.

methods_proposed.name: Four-stage annotation and generation pipeline
methods_proposed.summary: The authors collect 283 satirical images from the X handle @_yesbut_, annotate them with five annotators, then use DALL-E 3 to generate 2D and 3D stick-figure variants from the annotated descriptions and manually label the resulting combined images as satirical or non-satirical.

methods_proposed.name: Three-task satire comprehension benchmark
methods_proposed.summary: The paper defines Satirical Image Detection, Satirical Image Understanding, and Satirical Image Completion as benchmark tasks for evaluating zero-shot multimodal satire comprehension.

evaluation_methods.detail: Satirical Image Detection is evaluated with Accuracy and F1-Score; Satirical Image Completion is evaluated with accuracy; Satirical Image Understanding is evaluated with BLEU, ROUGE-L, METEOR, BERTScore, and, in the appendix, Polos.

For Satirical Image Understanding, 30 images are randomly sampled, 10 from each of Stages 2, 3, and 4; three students evaluate model-generated and human-written overall descriptions by majority vote on Correctness, Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness.
For the 119 real satirical photographs, Understanding is evaluated by whether the model-generated text correctly describes the satire in the image, and accuracy is reported for each VL Model.

headline_results.finding: On Satirical Image Detection, Kosmos-2 had the best reported TEST ACC. in the zero-shot CoT setting and the best F1 SCORE in the zero-shot setting; no model crossed 60 on either metric.
headline_results.best_system: Kosmos-2 (0-shot, CoT) for TEST ACC.; Kosmos-2 (0-shot) for F1 SCORE

headline_results.finding: For the central WHYFUNNY prompt in Satirical Image Understanding, the best Average Score in Table 6 was achieved by Gemini, while the paper states that all normalized average metric values are below 0.4.
In human evaluation of overall image descriptions, the best model scores were far below human-written descriptions across all four criteria.
headline_results.value: Correctness: 60.00 vs Human 100.00; Appropriate Length: 56.67 vs Human 100.00; Visual Completeness: 46.67 vs Human 80.00; Faithfulness: 56.67 vs Human 93.33
headline_results.best_system: GPT4 for Correctness; Gemini for Appropriate Length, Visual Completeness, and Faithfulness

headline_results.finding: On Satirical Image Completion, Gemini performed best in both zero-shot and zero-shot CoT settings.
On the 119 real satirical photographs, GPT4 performed best on both Detection and Understanding, but all models had Understanding accuracy below 50%.

key_findings: YesBut contains 2,547 images: 1,084 satirical and 1,463 non-satirical, across colorized sketch, 2D stick figure, and 3D stick figure styles.
The original 283 satirical images have 53.0% with zero sub-images containing text, 94.7% with unconnected sub-images, and 13.5% whose annotations were MEDIUM or HARD.
Satirical Image Detection is difficult for the evaluated VL models: the best test accuracy is 56.97 and the best F1 score is 59.71.
Zero-shot CoT has mixed effects for detection, improving test accuracy in only 2/5 models and F1 Score in only 1/5 models, but improves completion accuracy in 3/5 models.
For Satirical Image Understanding, the paper reports that all normalized average metric values are below 0.4 and that the best model remains substantially below human performance in human evaluation.
The appendix reports that models perform better on images with text in 15 out of 20 metric/model cases, suggesting that absence of text makes satire understanding harder.
limitations: The annotation task requires background knowledge that may differ among annotators, and although annotations were manually reviewed, some subjectivity still remains.
The work is in English, and the authors state that they plan to extend it to languages other than English.
safety_ethics_notes: The paper states that the 283 images from the X handle @_yesbut_ were manually downloaded “with proper consent.”
